faq.question: What is a wood clad window?
faq.answer: Wood Clad windows are built around a wood-clad frame system, chosen for a real-wood interior with a weather-resistant protective exterior shell. In practice the choice covers frame construction, glazing package and finish, all of which Deluxe Windows documents in each product proposal.

faq.question: How long do wood clad windows last?
faq.answer: Wood Clad windows and doors typically serve 30-50 years when the cladding and seals are maintained. Quality of installation, sun exposure and upkeep move products to either end of that range, so seals and hardware should be checked periodically.

faq.question: Are wood-clad windows better than vinyl?
faq.answer: Wood-clad offers a real-wood interior with a weather-resistant protective exterior shell at a premium over bare wood, offset by much lower exterior upkeep, while vinyl brings low maintenance, good insulation and the widest choice of budget-friendly series and typically lasts 20-40 years. The better fit depends on budget, architecture and how long you plan to keep the home.

faq.question: How much do wood clad windows cost installed in the Bay Area?
faq.answer: For wood clad windows, the current promotion prices installations from $549 per window installed (regular $915). The final quote reflects access, removal and finishing, and Deluxe Windows confirms exact installed pricing after measuring.

faq.question: Is removal of the old windows included when replacing with wood clad windows?
faq.answer: Yes — removal and disposal of the old windows are part of the wood clad windows installation scope, along with insulation, sealing and a final operating check. Everything is itemized in the written Bay Area quote.
